summary: Clear soundboard recording of a solid 1973 show, highlighted by an excellent Eyes > Stella jam and a rare one‑drummer Black Peter.
review: The remastered tape offers a clean, well‑balanced stereo image with good instrument presence, though a brief dropout is noted at the end of Dark Star. Musically the concert is tight and energetic, featuring an especially strong Eyes > Stella sequence and a coveted one‑drummer Black Peter performance, even if the rest of the set feels more typical than spectacular for the year.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews praise the recording’s clarity and note only minor issues, while describing the show as good with a few standout moments; weighted average yields a rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and clean soundboard capture with strong stereo image and instrument presence; minor dropout at the end of Dark Star noted but otherwise very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is cleanly played with good energy, a nice middle‑set flow and an extended jam on Eyes that stands out; other numbers are solid but not uniquely memorable for the era.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil's bass is described as warm and nicely present in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: A rare one‑drummer Black Peter highlight is noted, underscoring Bill Kreutzmann’s performance.
